phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight edge at level 1: your rewind dive resets beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 the numbers lean your way — rewind dive resets edge out the wall-frost poke control when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your rewind dive resets beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: E blink-strike window opens: Q-E-auto is your first real trade and the third passive hit stings already.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W zone games begin: drop it on his retreat line BEFORE trading and the stun finds him mid-regret.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your rewind dive resets out-muscle Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint your rewind dive resets beat the stardust ramp control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Aurelion Sol answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: First item

phases.why: Late Ekko picks and survives: dive the carry, W the peel, and Chronobreak out of whatever they answered with.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave and his toes — the return pass chunks; learn the slow zone's drag on his dodge patterns.

phases.why: W zone games begin: drop it on his retreat line BEFORE trading and the stun finds him mid-regret.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Three-hit trades on cooldown — Q in, E the gap, passive proc, walk. Bank the tempo for 6.

phases.why: Chronobreak online — your aggression gets a save file. Trade like the rewind exists, because it does.

phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Irelia's wave-dash blade stacks edge out your rewind dive resets on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the full string deletes half bars and the rewind erases the counter-argument.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight edge at two-plus items: your rewind dive resets beat Irelia's wave-dash blade stacks on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the wave and his toes — the return pass chunks; learn the slow zone's drag on his dodge patterns.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: E blink-strike window opens: Q-E-auto is your first real trade and the third passive hit stings already.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: W zone games begin: drop it on his retreat line BEFORE trading and the stun finds him mid-regret.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Three-hit trades on cooldown — Q in, E the gap, passive proc, walk. Bank the tempo for 6.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Chronobreak online — your aggression gets a save file. Trade like the rewind exists, because it does.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your rewind dive resets beat Pantheon's point-click spear bullying on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items your rewind dive resets beat the point-click spear bullying in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Pantheon answer you, not the other way around.
